name: Pressure Plate
description: A trigger surface that changes state when a qualifying actor or object applies pressure.
design_notes: Pressure plates are readable puzzle inputs when their activation rules are explicit. The mechanic should define what counts as weight, whether activation is binary or analog, how long the state persists, and what output it controls. Good plates teach cause and effect before asking for timing or multi-object reasoning.
design_purpose: Let players activate world state through body position, objects, or weight.
Create puzzles around placement, timing, cooperation, and object transport.
Make cause-and-effect relationships visible in the environment.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are manipulating a physical space where weight and position matter.

parameters.name: activation_mass_threshold
parameters.description: Combined qualifying mass required to activate the plate.

parameters.name: plate_depress_depth
parameters.description: Visual movement used when the plate activates.

parameters.name: release_linger_ms
parameters.description: Delay before deactivation after pressure is removed.

parameters.name: output_channel_id
parameters.description: Puzzle signal or channel controlled by the plate.

edge_cases: Stacked objects should specify whether mass adds, only top object counts, or each collider counts separately.
Objects resting partly on the plate need stable contribution rules to avoid flicker.
Physics sleep should not prevent a resting object from keeping the plate active.
Save/load must restore both depressed visual state and linked puzzle output.
common_bugs: Multiple colliders on one object count as multiple objects and over-activate the plate.
The output toggles every frame instead of changing only on state transitions.
A destroyed object never sends exit, leaving the plate permanently active.
Networked co-op clients disagree about activation because physics authority is not centralized.
balancing_notes: Visible wiring, sound, or camera framing should show what the plate controls before puzzles become complex.
Release delays can make timing puzzles more forgiving without changing the visible rule.
accessibility_notes: Use shape, motion, sound, and color together to show plate activation.
Avoid puzzles that require holding a plate and reading distant results without camera or UI support.

implementation_notes.unity: Use trigger colliders with a tracked set of qualifying rigidbodies or actors; aggregate mass by object identity rather than collider count.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area2D or Area3D enter/exit signals, store qualifying bodies in a set, and emit puzzle-state changes only when activation changes.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use overlap volumes with Gameplay Tags or interfaces for qualifying actors; replicate activation state rather than raw overlap events in multiplayer.
implementation_notes.web: Use collision overlap queries against tagged entities and keep plate state as data that emits events only on threshold crossings.
